entities.name: SEMI-ARID INDIA
entities.description: Semi-arid regions of India where integrated watershed management is applied

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Tropical regions characterized by semi-arid climates

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability of agricultural systems to maintain productivity over time

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving agricultural productivity and livelihoods

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCES
entities.description: Environmental assets like land and water exploited for livelihoods

entities.name: SEMI-ARID AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and water scarcity

entities.name: WATER SCARCITY
entities.description: Lack of sufficient water resources for agricultural needs

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Prolonged periods of abnormally low rainfall

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: Decline in soil quality and fertility

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of an agricultural system to recover from disturbances

entities.name: INTEGRATED WATERSHED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A community-based strategy for sustainable land and water management

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE INTENSIFICATION OF AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Increasing agricultural yields without compromising environmental sustainability

entities.name: WATERSHED
entities.description: A land area that channels rainfall and snowmelt to creeks, streams, and rivers

entities.name: HYDROLOGICAL UNIT
entities.description: A geographic area defined by its water drainage characteristics

entities.name: COMMUNITY
entities.description: The social group involved in collective action for resource management

entities.name: INSTITUTIONS
entities.description: Social structures and rules governing the management of resources

relationships.description: Degradation of agroecosystems leads to declining sustainability
Livelihoods for agricultural development depend on exploitation of natural resources
Semi-arid areas are characterized by water scarcity
Water scarcity lowers the resilience of the system
Drought lowers the resilience of the system
Soil degradation lowers the resilience of the system
IWM is a strategy for improving agricultural productivity
IWM is a strategy for sustainable intensification of agriculture
IWM utilizes the biophysical notion of a watershed
IWM incorporates the social aspect of community
IWM utilizes community institutions for sustainable management
A watershed is defined as a hydrological unit
